heading: Homestead, mineral, and other rights un­affected
label: 247 Homestead, mineral, and other rights un­affected
text: That nothing herein contained shall affect any valid existing claim, location, or entry under the land laws of the United States, whether for homestead, mineral, right-of-way, or any other purposes whatsoever, or shall affect the right of any such claimant, locator, or entryman to the full use and enjoyment of his land.
notes: References in TextHerein, referred to in text, means act , which was generally classified to this subchapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.Apr. 25, 1947The land laws of the United States, referred to in text, are classified generally to Title 43, Public Lands.
